stimulus: Sicily, the central Mediterranean island that now sits at the southern tip of Italy, was governed by various Arabic-speaking emirates over roughly two centuries beginning in the ninth century. ______blank many Arabic words, such as "zibibbo"—the name of a grape variety still cultivated on the island—worked their way into the Sicilian language.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. "Consequently" logically signals that the information in this sentence—that many Arabic words entered the Sicilian language—is a result of the previous information about Sicily being governed by Arabic-speaking emirates for roughly two centuries.

Choice A is incorrect because "instead" illogically signals that the information about Arabic loanwords is an alternative to the previous information about the emirates' rule, when it is in fact a consequence of that rule. Choice B is incorrect because "specifically" illogically signals that this sentence provides specific details about the emirates' rule, when it actually describes a downstream linguistic consequence. Choice C is incorrect because "for example" illogically signals that the entry of Arabic loanwords exemplifies the emirates' rule, when it is actually a consequence of that rule.
